steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Count the relevant faces, dimensions, or scale factor before setting up equations. Lengths scale by $k$, areas by $k^2$, and volumes by $k^3$; for boxes and prisms, write the needed face or volume formula directly.

steps.title: Find the radius of container B
steps.content:
Container A has radius 12 cm. Container B's radius is 25% longer:
$$r_B = 12 + 0.25(12) = 12 + 3 = 15 \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Calculate volume of container B
steps.content:
Substituting $r = 15$ cm and $h = 60$ cm into the cylinder volume formula:
$$V = \pi(15)^2(60) = \pi(225)(60) = 13{,}500\pi \text{ cm}^3$$
That matches Choice B — $13 500 \pi$.
